title: Where did "money is hard" come from?
subtitle: A journalling set for tracing the belief that earning requires struggle.
excerpt: A journal prompt set for examining the hard-earning belief: where it came from, who modelled it, whether it was ever true, and what a lighter version of earning might look like for you. Five prompts, twenty to thirty minutes.
sourceNotes: Drawn from The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025), Week 2 prompts on breaking generational money patterns.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you notice yourself overworking, undercharging, or making earning harder than it needs to be.

content.text: A journal set for tracing the hard-earning belief back to its source. Five prompts. Allow twenty to thirty minutes.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
What does earning currently feel like in your body?
Heavy, light, neutral, anxious? Write what's true right now, not what should be.
Did anyone in your family make earning look easy? Or was it always shown as a struggle?
Name the person and the picture you have of them and money.
Where did the belief that money is hard to earn first land?
A specific memory, a phrase overheard, a lesson taught directly. Write what you find.
Is that belief still serving you? Name one way it protects you and one way it costs you.
What would you charge, take, or accept if you believed earning could be lighter?
One specific thing. Name the number or the opportunity.
Drawn from The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025), Week 2.
